title: Perf: Rank action takes 2-4s — eliminate full vault scans from the write path
description: Confirmed in live testing 2026-07-02: clicking Rank takes ~2-4s. Causes: backlog_rank_task runs a full scan_noteplan_dir on every call (only to collect existing block IDs for collision checks), plus two sequential get_note MCP round-trips, plus the frontend reload() triggering another full vault scan via get_backlog. Fixes: cache/reuse the scanned NoteStore (or maintain an in-memory block-id set), batch the MCP calls where possible, and make the post-write UI update incremental instead of a full rescan. Was consciously deferred in review as 'non-hot-path' — it IS the hot path for the Backlog UX.
notes: RESOLVED-pending-merge (2026-07-03): rank total now 45-79ms (was 17-33s). Root cause was noteplan-mcp TITLE resolution (2-6s/call); filename addressing is 3-17ms/call. Round 1 (cache/suppression) + round 2 (7->4 calls, filename addressing, local cache patch) both on fix/rank-perf / PR #6. Bridge-backend guard being added, then merge.
close_reason: Merged PR #6: rank 33s -> ~50ms (filename addressing + 4 calls + cache + bridge guard)

title: Reconcile mcp/tools.rs with the REAL NotePlan MCP schema (blocks backlog writes)
description: MCP Inspector against @noteplanco/noteplan-mcp revealed tools.rs was written against an assumed schema. The Phase 2 backlog write path FAILS at runtime (fails safe — invalid action rejected, nothing written — but non-functional). Confirmed real schema: (1) noteplan_get_notes has NO 'action' param; needs includeContent:true; returns a JSON envelope with the body in .content (our get_note must PARSE .content, not return raw JSON); default limit 500 lines + hasMore (must request full note or paginate or abort-safe). Line base = 1, matches on-disk (resolves w3o line-base nit). (2) noteplan_edit_content actions are insert/append/delete_lines/edit_line/replace_lines (NOT replace/delete); content field is 'content' NOT 'text'. Confirmed edit_line{title,line,content} works and needs no confirmationToken. delete must be delete_lines{startLine,endLine}; insert needs position:'at-line'. (3) All edit tools return {success:bool,...} — we must parse success and return Err on failure (data-safety: detect failed writes). (4) Dead-but-wrong wrappers: manage_note move uses destinationFolder (not toFolder); ui action is open_note (not open); paragraphs search has no 'completed' (use status); eventkit action get_events + requires source. All destructive tools expose dryRun+confirmationToken (potential extra safety layer — TBD). Fix on feat/backlog-write-path before merging PR #4.
close_reason: tools.rs reconciled to real MCP schema; merged in PR #4

title: Build committed fixture vault for headless read-path dev + regression tests
description: Per tooling decision 2026-07-02: create a tests/fixture-vault/ (or similar) directory modeling a realistic NotePlan data dir: Notes/ with JD area/category folders (work + personal areas), _NotePlan Organizer/ with #np-projects and #np-backlog control notes, Calendar/ dailies, tasks covering every syntax edge (!/!!/!!! priorities, ^blockIds, >dates, <dates, [x]/[-]/[>] states, tags, mentions, bare - list items, + leaders), @Templates/@Archive/@Trash/_attachments exclusion cases, >1000-line note for the hasMore guard, duplicate titles, unresolved control-note refs. Wire integration tests that run scan_noteplan_dir + build_project_board + build_backlog against it. This becomes the standard harness for all future read-path work.
close_reason: Fixture vault + integration harness merged in PR #5

title: Epic: UI/UX overhaul — unify navigation, MCP connect flow, rebrand-era design pass
description: User feedback 2026-07-02 after Phase 2 landed: the stacked toggle rows (Findings|Assessment|Priorities|Filing|Tasks, then Board|Backlog, then Work|Home) are 'super funky'; the MCP connect UX (tiny status-tray toggle) is wonky/undiscoverable. Now that the app has outgrown its original findings-list identity (analysis + filing + task management), do a whole-app UX rethink: navigation structure (maybe sidebar), MCP connection flow (auto-connect? prominent state?), visual hierarchy. Coordinate with the rename (NotePlan Companion). Big effort — do a proper brainstorm/design phase first.

title: Rename app: NotePlan Organizer -> NotePlan Companion
description: User decision 2026-07-02: 'Organizer' fit when the app only policed the organization system; with task management (Priorities/Backlog) it's now a general companion. Rename touches: tauri.conf.json (productName, identifier, window title), package.json/Cargo.toml names, README, CLAUDE.md, header UI in App.tsx, repo name (turboladen/noteplan-organizer -> ?; GitHub redirects old URLs), beads prefix (bd rename-prefix — big, maybe skip), icons unchanged. Coordinate with the UX overhaul epic so branding lands once.

title: Audit pre-existing Tauri commands for camelCase IPC arg mismatch
description: Found while testing the backlog write path: Tauri v2 exposes command args to JS as camelCase by default, but src/api/commands.ts sends snake_case keys throughout, and NO pre-existing command uses rename_all. The new backlog commands are fixed (rename_all=snake_case, commit 03d4e14 on feat/backlog-write-path), but these pre-existing commands with multi-word args are likely broken on main today: get_content_blocks{note_path}, get_filing_suggestions{base_path,note_path}, export_assessment_context{guide_title}, mcp_call_tool{tool_name} — i.e. the Filing assistant tab and the FixAction 'Fix' button path may fail with 'missing required key'. Verify each in the running app, then annotate with #[tauri::command(rename_all="snake_case")] (matching the TS convention) and add a regression check. Single-word args (path, url, line) are unaffected.

title: Board/Backlog: load from predefined project folder on demand, no full-vault scan or Scan-button gate
description:
Two compounding inefficiencies keep the Board/Backlog tab from being usable until the user clicks "Scan Notes":

1. Frontend gate: in App.tsx, the `activeTab === "priorities"` block that renders <ProjectBoard> only exists inside the `{report && (...)}` wrapper (around line 575). `report` is only set by `handleScan()`, which invokes the full `scan` Tauri command. So opening the Board tab first requires running the entire structural-analysis scan (all 8 analyzers via run_all_analyzers), even though the Board doesn't use `report.findings` at all.

2. Backend full-vault walk: independent of the UI gate, `get_project_board` (commands.rs) itself calls `scan_noteplan_dir(&path)`, which recursively parses every note in the whole NotePlan data directory. This is only to (a) find the `#np-projects` control note by tag search across all Regular notes (parser/projects.rs parse_project_control), and (b) resolve the project references it lists to their target notes/folders (resolve_folder). Neither step needs the rest of the vault (daily notes, unrelated JD categories, etc.) — the board should be able to open by reading just the control note plus the specific JD-category project folders it references.

Goal: the Board/Backlog view should populate on tab open (or app load) without requiring "Scan Notes" first, and the read path should be scoped to the known project-note location(s) rather than a full recursive scan_noteplan_dir call.

Open design question (no existing convention for this yet — checked config.rs, nothing there): how do we scope the read? Options to weigh: (a) require the #np-projects control note to live at a known top-level path so we can read just that file directly instead of tag-scanning all notes, then only recurse into the referenced JD-category folders; (b) keep tag discovery but cache/limit it to top-level notes; (c) something else. Needs a short design pass before implementation — this is a change to the read path used by both Phase 1 (read-only board, already shipped) and Phase 2 (backlog writes, in progress in ays).

Touches: src-tauri/src/parser/projects.rs (parse_project_control, build_project_board, resolve_folder), src-tauri/src/parser/mod.rs (scan_noteplan_dir), src-tauri/src/commands.rs (get_project_board), src/App.tsx (report-gated rendering of ProjectBoard/activeTab === "priorities").

Coordinate with ays (Phase 2 backlog write path, in progress) before starting — likely touches overlapping parser/projects.rs code.

title: Consolidate NotePlan line parsing into one shared tokenizer
description: FIRST task of Phase 2 (do before backlog.rs/backlog_write.rs are written). NotePlan task/line syntax is currently parsed in multiple places with subtly different rules (task.rs vs block.rs already diverge; Phase 2 would add copies in backlog.rs + backlog_write.rs planners). This is a data-safety risk: if a line is tokenized one way for display and another for verify-before-write, that mismatch is exactly what causes wrong-line writes. Extract a single hand-written parse_task_line(raw) -> ParsedTaskLine { leader, state, display_text, priority, block_id, tags, mentions, scheduled_to, rescheduled_from } and have task.rs, block.rs, backlog.rs, and the write planners all call it. NOT a Markdown AST lib (pulldown-cmark/comrak) — those normalize away the exact source offsets/line fidelity the line-addressed MCP write path depends on. No new dependency. Decision recorded 2026-07-01 (chose consolidate/hand-written over combinator lib or deferral). Then revise Phase 2 plan so backlog.rs/backlog_write.rs consume the shared tokenizer.
close_reason: Parser consolidation merged in PR #3

title: Add dprint catch-all + Prettier (React) + a format CI gate (none exists yet)
description:
Standardize formatting on the cross-repo model — kammerz is the reference: native tools own their domains, dprint is the catch-all for everything else. noteplan-organizer is the least-set-up of the four.

## Current state
- React 19 + Tauri + Vite (bun). Rust lives in `src-tauri/`.
- **Only eslint** (`bun run lint`) — no formatter at all: no dprint, no prettier.
- **No GitHub Actions CI at all** (no `.github/` directory). Has a `Justfile`.

## Target (match kammerz exactly)
- Add `dprint.jsonc` catch-all: `json` + `markdown` + `toml` + `pretty_yaml` plugins; exclude `node_modules`, `dist`, `src-tauri/target`, `.beads`, `.claude`. Pin plugin versions (align with kammerz).
- Add a native React/TS formatter — **Prettier** (matches kammerz; Biome is an acceptable alt). eslint stays for linting only.
- Rust (`src-tauri/`) → `cargo fmt`.
- Wire `just fmt` / `just fmt-check` in the existing Justfile to run all three (dprint + prettier + cargo fmt).
- **CI: none exists** — "update CI" here means *add* a minimal `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with at least a format gate (`just fmt-check`). Broader lint/test/build CI can be a follow-up bead.

Reference: kammerz `dprint.jsonc`, `frontend/.prettierrc`, `justfile` fmt/fmt-check targets, and CI `format` job.
acceptance_criteria: 'just fmt-check' runs dprint (md/json/toml/yaml) + prettier (React/TS) + cargo fmt --check clean; a new .github/workflows/ci.yml format job enforces it on PRs.

First feature that writes to NotePlan. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-priority-board-phase2-backlog-writes.md

title: Phase 1: parsing foundation + read-only Board

close_reason: Phase 1 read-only Board merged in PR #2

title: Epic: Project & Task Priority Board
description: Read-only project Board (rolls up tasks per JD-category project, ranked via #np-projects control note, sliced by context) + drag-to-reorder Backlog (per-context manual task ranking anchored to native NotePlan ^blockIds, written back via MCP under strict data-safety invariants). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-project-priority-board-design.md. Plans: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-priority-board-phase{1,2}-*.md

title: Exclude base 'Memo' folder (Memo AI sync) from analysis
description:
A base 'Memo' folder is now used to sync tasks into NotePlan from the 'Memo AI' app. These notes are not part of the JD organization hierarchy and should not be scanned/assessed — otherwise ID Consistency (and other analyzers) will flag them for missing/invalid IDs, missing hubs, being unfiled, etc.

Currently the folder-exclusion check ('@Archive', '@Trash', '@Templates', '_attachments') is duplicated inline via relative_path.contains(...) across ~12 analyzers plus parser/filing.rs and parser/hierarchy.rs. 'Memo' is not excluded anywhere.
design: The exclusion predicate is copy-pasted in ~14 sites. Prefer adding 'Memo' to a single shared helper (e.g. an is_excluded_path(relative_path) fn) and having analyzers call it, rather than pasting a 15th contains() check into each file. If a shared helper doesn't exist yet, introducing one is in scope and reduces future drift. Match on a leading path segment (e.g. starts_with("Memo/") / equals "Memo") rather than contains("Memo") to avoid false exclusions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Notes under a top-level 'Memo' folder are excluded from ID Consistency and all other analyzers, matching how @Archive/@Trash/_attachments are skipped
- Exclusion matches the base 'Memo' folder specifically (avoid over-matching notes that merely contain 'memo' in a path segment/title)
- Existing exclusions unchanged; add/adjust a test covering a Memo note being skipped
- cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passes
notes: Sites using the current exclusion: analyzer/{id_consistency,stale_area,cross_wired_id,missing_hub,duplicates,broken_links,template_placeholders,hub_completeness,orphaned_notes,empty_structure,naming_inconsistency,unfiled}.rs and parser/{filing,hierarchy}.rs. Broader than ID Consistency but surfaced by the same org-scheme change.

title: ID Consistency: flag single-dot old-style hierarchical note IDs (incl. [Parent].00 old hubs)
description:
Org scheme changed: hubs are now '00.{PH,DH,RH} - Title' (HubCode) and notes use bare sequential IDs or ISO8601 dates (mixing is fine). The previous hub form '[Parent].00 - Title' (e.g. 10.00 - Hub) and old child IDs '[Parent].NN' (e.g. 10.02) are now unacceptable and should be flagged for migration.

The parser already classifies HubCode / DatePrefix / Sequential / BareHub correctly. The gap is in analyzer/id_consistency.rs: the JdDotted branch only flags note_id where dot_count >= 2 (three-segment like 10.01.01) AND note_id.starts_with(parent_prefix). Single-dot legacy IDs — the most common old form — are silently accepted:
  - '10.00 - Hub'  -> JdDotted, dot_count=1 -> NOT flagged (old hub, should become 00.PH/DH/RH)
  - '10.02 - Foo'  -> JdDotted, dot_count=1 -> NOT flagged (should become '02' or a date)

Also the parent_prefix gate means a dotted ID whose parent folder has no JD id (parent_jd_id=None) is never flagged, even though any dotted note ID is old-style under the new scheme.
design: Old hierarchical style is unacceptable across the board now, so flag ALL JdDotted note IDs rather than thresholding on depth. Special-case the '.00' suffix to point at the hub-code form since those are old hubs. Recommend bumping severity from Info to Warning for old hubs (bare 00 is already Error; hubs are structurally important); plain dotted children can stay Info or Warning — implementer's call, note it in the PR.
acceptance_criteria:
- Any Regular-note title ID classified as JdDotted is flagged (dot_count >= 1), not just >= 2
- Drop/relax the note_id.starts_with(parent_prefix) gate so dotted IDs are flagged regardless of parent folder id
- '[Parent].00' (ends in .00) suggests migrating to '00.PH' / '00.DH' / '00.RH'
- Other dotted IDs suggest migrating to a bare sequential id or ISO8601 date
- HubCode (00.PH/DH/RH), DatePrefix, and 2+ digit Sequential remain unflagged; bare '00' stays Error
- Unit tests added: single-dot old hub (10.00) flagged with hub-code suggestion; single-dot child (10.02) flagged with sequential/date suggestion; existing 3-segment and stale-filename tests still pass
- cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passes
notes: Relevant files: src-tauri/src/analyzer/id_consistency.rs (lines ~56-87 JdDotted branch), src-tauri/src/parser/folder.rs::parse_note_id, parent_jd_id_from_path. References Hub (00.RH in '5x - References') already works via HubCode — no change needed for the References section.

title: Add config flag for parsing backend
description: Settings UI toggle: Rust (fast, file-based) vs MCP (NotePlan API). Only if benchmarks show MCP is viable. Depends on MCP integration and benchmark beads.
notes: Benchmark results (2026-03-16): Rust full parse ~1200ms for ~1600 notes. MCP list-only ~230ms, MCP get ~1ms/note (~1.6s total). MCP retrieval is competitive on raw I/O but doesn't include structural parsing — Rust parser still wins for bulk scanning. Config toggle is low-value: keep Rust for scanning, MCP for targeted ops (writes, search, embeddings). Recommend deferring or closing as won't-do.
close_reason: Benchmark shows Rust parser wins for bulk scanning. MCP get is fast (~1ms/note) but still needs Rust parsing on top. Config toggle adds complexity for no user benefit.

title: Benchmark Rust file parsing vs MCP note retrieval
description: Compare scan times for ~1600 notes using current Rust parser vs MCP noteplan_get_notes. Measure latency, throughput, and memory. Results inform whether a config toggle is worth adding.
close_reason: Benchmark command compares Rust scan_noteplan_dir vs MCP list + sample get. Results shown via toast. Config toggle (bead 3iz) decision deferred to after real-world benchmarking.

title: Scope watcher suppression to app-written paths (or trailing rescan on window close)
description: Fast follow-up from the rank-perf review (fix/rank-perf). The 4s watcher-suppression window is GLOBAL: any FS event during it is dropped, so a user edit to an UNRELATED note during the window isn't re-analyzed until the next FS event or manual rescan. Display/analysis staleness only — never corruption (the app never writes from watcher-derived state; the cache refresh patches the touched notes). Fix options: (a) scope suppression to the specific paths apply_ops wrote (needs title->path resolution), or (b) simpler: fire one trailing debounced rescan when the suppression window closes. Reviewer recommended (b)-style follow-up as sufficient.

title: Backlog rank: harden micro-window residuals (stale entry + get_note line-base abort)
description: Phase 2 backlog follow-ups from the write-path review (non-corrupting, deferred). (1) STALE ENTRY RACE: if the target task gets stamped with a different id in the sub-second window between the command's planning snapshot and the executor's fresh get_note, the executor correctly skips the double content-write (idempotent), but the backlog InsertBacklogLine — built at planning time with the planning-era block_id — still inserts an entry referencing the now-orphan id -> a stale/unresolved backlog entry on next read. App-owned note only, never content destruction, recoverable (reader shows it as stale; user removes/re-ranks). Fix would couple the stamp+insert ops through the executor so the backlog entry uses the id actually on the line. (2) get_note LINE-BASE NIT: plan_stamp_block_id still does a snapshot-line check against the frontend's on-disk line; if MCP get_note's line base differs from the on-disk scan it can spuriously abort a valid rank (user retries). Robustness, not safety — the authoritative gate is now content relocation (locate_unique_task_line). Consider dropping/relaxing the snapshot-line pre-check now that relocate-by-content is authoritative.
notes: Round-2 additions (2026-07-03, both non-corrupting fast-follows from the single-fetch review): (a) write-time idempotency re-check dropped — a concurrent stamp of the exact same line within the one-round-trip window gets its ^id overwritten (task text fully preserved; old backlog entry goes stale). (b) partial-failure collision-miss — cache patches only on full write success, so a stamp that landed before a later op failed isn't in the collision set until rescan; an immediate next rank could emit a duplicate id (read-side mis-resolution only). Options: on failure drop affected cache entries or trigger a scan. Original item 1 (stale-entry race) unchanged.

title: resolve_folder: bare non-JD refs can match nested subfolder by order
description: Phase 1 follow-up (flagged in review). parser/projects.rs resolve_folder() iterates notes and returns the first folder whose final segment matches the ref name (case-insensitive) or leading JD id. For a bare, non-JD reference (no numeric prefix) that collides with a nested subfolder name, resolution is order-dependent and may pick the wrong folder. Not triggered by the documented usage (JD-prefixed category refs like '32 - Product Ownership'). Fix options: prefer shallowest/topmost matching folder, or require JD-id match for disambiguation, or match against known JD-category folders only. Low priority.

title: Semantic link suggestions (Phase 3)
